opening: THE HOLY AND GLORIOUS APOSTLE THOMAS

troparia.label: Troparion (8): You came down from on high, O Merciful One, and accepted three days of burial to free us from our
troparia.text: sufferings. O Lord, our life and our resurrection, glory be to You.

troparia.label: Troparion (2): O disciple of Christ and member of the apostolic council, your lack of faith proclaimed Christ's
troparia.text:
resurrection, and your touch confirmed his His holy passion. O Thomas, worthy of all praise, pray even now that He
grant us peace and great mercy.
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit. Now and for ever and ever: Amen.

kondakia.label: Kontakion (4): Filled with the grace of wisdom, Your apostle and genuine servant, O Christ, cried out to You in his
kondakia.text: change of heart: You are my God and Lord.

prokeimenon.text: Verse: In Judea God is known; His name is great in Israel.

epistle.text:
Reading of the Second Epistle of St. Paul to the Corinthians.
(2Cor 6,16-18;7,1)
Brethren: We are the temple of the living God; as God said: “I will live with them and move among them, and I will be
their God and they shall be my people. Therefore, come forth from them and be separate,” says the Lord, “and touch
nothing unclean; then I will receive you and I will be a father to you, and you shall be sons and daughters to me, says
the Lord Almighty.” Since we have these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from every defilement of flesh
and spirit, making holiness perfect in the fear of God.

verses: Verse (8): Come, let us rejoice in the Lord; let us acclaim God our Savior:

gospel.text:
(Lk 7,11-16)
TWENTIETH SUNDAY AFTER PENTECOST
At that time, Jesus journeyed to a city called Nain, and his disciples and a large crowd accompanied him. As he drew
near to the gate of the city, a man who had died was being carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a
widow. A large crowd from the city was with her. When the Lord saw her, he was moved with pity for her and said to
her, “Do not weep.” He stepped forward and touched the coffin; at this the bearers halted, and he said, “Young man, I
tell you, arise!” The dead man sat up and began to speak, and Jesus gave him to his mother. Fear seized them all, and
they glorified God, exclaiming, “A great prophet has arisen in our midst,” and “God has visited his people.”

communionHymn:
Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest.
Their utterance has gone forth into all the earth, and their words unto the ends of the world.
